title: Collections, Taxonomy, Ordering, Navigation, Feeds, and Sitemaps · Changed-Input Lab
summary: An independent documentation team receives a different content tree, language, route base, template, asset set, trust policy, hosting target, and accessibility need and transfers collections, taxonomy, ordering, navigation, feeds, and sitemaps into a changed reconciled accessible site graph and derived publications. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of reconciled accessible site graph and derived publications with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress…
objectives: Select published documents by typed metadata, language, route, taxonomy, and time under deterministic query and ordering rules.
Normalize tag and category identity separately from labels, aliases, hierarchy, route, and language.
Build global, local, breadcrumb, previous-next, related, and multiple-way navigation from an acyclic visible route graph.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Collection Query
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab publishing team handles case 1497 during a compromised-content recovery rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile navigation, taxonomy, feeds, and sitemaps to one published route manifest; reconcile content, routes, navigation, links, assets, feeds, sitemaps, output files, and artifact digests before approval; then publish below a project subpath and verify every internal URL, fragment, asset, feed, and canonical. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Collection Query.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for select published documents by typed metadata, language, route, taxonomy, and time under deterministic query and ordering rules. For Static-site publishing and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Collection Query, keep Collection Query connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

Filtering a list of dictionaries is enough to define a stable collection. That claim fails the commit to the mission model evidence boundary for Collection Query.

For commit to the mission model, render one trusted short page at the root, omit content authority, source spans, parser limits, route base, URL and asset reconciliation, contextual encoding, accessibility, clean-build comparison, deployment, rollback, recovery, and production transfer, then accept plausible HTML as publication proof. This does not satisfy Collection Query for reconciled accessible site graph and derived publications.

steps.instruction:

Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, cache, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, CommonMark 0.31.2 fixtures, markdown-it-py 4.2.0, Jinja 3.1.6 strict autoescape, pytest 9.1.1, standards validators, real browsers, clean package installs, reproducible artifacts, staged deployment, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-feed-sitemap-u31yig
# Competency: ssg-feed-sitemap.
# Case 1227: a compromised-content recovery rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, reader task, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: rename a titled source while preserving its public identity and redirect evidence.
def evidence_ssg_feed_sitemap_u31yig(published: int = 13, navigable: int = 13, taxonomy_terms: int = 5, feed_entries: int = 13, sitemap_urls: int = 13) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_u31yig = "u31yig-giy13u-1227"
    assert evidence_variant_u31yig
    evidence_axes_u31yig = (
        "readeru31yig",
        "source1227",
        "parsegiy13u",
        "routeu31yig1227",
        "artifact1227u31yig",
        "recoverygiy13u1227",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_u31yig)) == 6
    if published < 1 or navigable < 0:
        return False, navigable, "publication-count"
    if navigable != published:
        return False, navigable, "orphan-route"
    if taxonomy_terms < 1:
        return False, taxonomy_terms, "taxonomy-shape"
    if feed_entries != published or sitemap_urls != published:
        return False, feed_entries, "derived-publication-drift"
    assert feed_entries == sitemap_urls == published
    return True, navigable, "reconciled-site-graph"
